name: Imperial
description_short: Imperial Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving families of the African Methodist Episcopal Church in Illinois and Iowa, offering savings accounts, small loans, and gift cards.
description_long:
Imperial Credit Union was founded to serve members and families of the African Methodist Episcopal (AME) Church of the Illinois Conference Branch, which includes parts of Iowa. It is recognized as the oldest continuously African American-owned business based in Springfield, Illinois. The credit union operates as a volunteer organization dedicated to providing financial services to underserved and unserved communities, emphasizing accessibility and community support rather than profit maximization.

Imperial offers several core financial products including NCUA-insured savings accounts, share-secured loans for members with $500+ in deposits, a 10/10 loan program ($1,000 at 10% interest with 12-month repayment terms), and gift cards ranging from $10–$1,000 for personal and online use. Members can access their accounts and make withdrawals through phone calls, email, or PayPal transfers. The credit union prioritizes member education, publishing guidance on emergency savings, financial stability, and sound spending habits.

Imperial distinguishes itself through its mission-driven focus on serving a specific faith community and historically underbanked populations. Unlike traditional banks, the credit union does not check credit scores for share-secured loans, making it accessible to members with credit challenges. Its volunteer structure and local Springfield roots create a personalized, relationship-based banking experience. The organization emphasizes teaching financial literacy and has deep roots in Black American business history.

The primary limitation is that Imperial's membership is restricted to members and families of the AME Church in the Illinois Conference Branch. The organization's volunteer status and small scale may limit service hours, product variety, and technology infrastructure compared to larger financial institutions. Potential members should verify eligibility and contact the credit union directly at 217-481-6381 or info@imperialcreditunion.org for current account opening requirements and service details.
services: NCUA-insured savings accounts with deposits protected up to $250,000
Share-secured loans ($500–$2,500) at 5% interest for members with $500+ in deposits
10/10 loan program—$1,000 at 10% APR with up to 12 months repayment
Digital and physical gift cards ($10–$1,000) for personal and online use
Withdrawal services via phone (217-481-6381), email, or PayPal
Financial education resources on saving, emergency funds, and sound financial habits
Volunteer leadership and community-focused advisory services
pros: NCUA insurance protects deposits up to $250,000—no member has ever lost money in an NCUA-insured account
Share-secured loans available to members with $500+ in savings at 5% interest without credit checks
No credit score damage from borrowing since the credit union does not check credit reports
10/10 loan program offers $1,000 at 10% APR with flexible 12-month repayment for qualified members
Oldest continuously African American-owned business in Springfield, Illinois with deep community roots
Multiple withdrawal methods including phone, email, and PayPal for accessibility
Volunteer-driven organization with mission focus on underserved communities rather than profit extraction
cons: Membership is restricted to members and families of the AME Church Illinois Conference Branch—not open to the general public
Limited product offerings compared to larger credit unions and banks (no mention of checking accounts, debit cards, or online banking platforms)
Volunteer-based structure may result in limited service hours and slower response times than larger financial institutions
Small scale and limited technology infrastructure may restrict digital banking capabilities
Share-secured loan maximum of $2,500 and savings requirement of $500 limits access for those with minimal deposits
best_for: AME Church members and their families seeking safe, mission-aligned savings and lending
Individuals with limited credit history or poor credit scores who need loans without credit checks
Community members seeking personalized banking with emphasis on financial education and stability
Those wanting to support the oldest continuously African American-owned business in Springfield
diagnosis: Imperial Credit Union is best for members and families of the AME Church seeking safe, accessible savings and small loans with a mission-driven, community-focused approach. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to the AME Church community in the Illinois Conference Branch, and the organization's volunteer structure limits service hours and digital capabilities compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
